seo.title: AI Agent Observability in Haystack Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent observability in Haystack agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Haystack, AI Agent Observability challenges arise from opaque pipeline failures where components like retrievers and generators silently produce low-quality outputs or crash without traces. Pipeline type mismatches and custom component errors get swallowed by the framework's async execution, making debugging production agents nearly impossible. Vex integration as a custom component provides end-to-end tracing of these failures specific to Haystack's Pipeline.run() patterns.

howItManifests.symptom: DocumentSearchReader expects Document objects but receives dicts from custom retriever
howItManifests.impact: Pipeline.run() raises cryptic TypeError deep in async execution stack

howItManifests.symptom: RAG pipeline returns top-k docs with cosine similarity < 0.3 despite query relevance
howItManifests.impact: Generator produces hallucinated answers citing irrelevant context

howItManifests.symptom: Generated response contains facts not present in input Documents.metadata
howItManifests.impact: End-users receive confidently wrong answers from production agents

howItManifests.symptom: User-defined Index silently fails during add_records() without Pipeline feedback
howItManifests.impact: Agent appears functional but serves stale/empty search results

detection.description: Flags retrievers returning documents below quality threshold

fixes.name: Schema-Enforced Pipeline Components
fixes.description: Uses Haystack's @component.output_types() to prevent type mismatches

fixes.name: Fallback Reranker Integration
Error-Bubbling Custom Components
fixes.description: Ensures custom components propagate errors to Pipeline.run()

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Observability Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a zero-config Haystack component that automatically instruments all Pipeline.run() calls, capturing component inputs/outputs, latency, and quality metrics without code changes. It detects the four core Haystack failure modes through runtime schema validation, relevance scoring, and hallucination pattern matching trained specifically on Haystack Document objects and pipeline traces.

Vex's agent creates remediation pipelines that inject rerankers, type converters, and context enforcers exactly where failures occur. Production Haystack agents running through Vex achieve 95%+ uptime with automatic root cause analysis linking retriever drift to generator hallucinations.

The Vex SDK hooks into Haystack's component graph, providing span-based observability that traces errors from custom Index.add_records() through Generator.run() without async deadlocks.
vexAutomation.code:
from haystack import Pipeline
from vex_sdk.haystack import VexObserver
from vex_sdk.types import HaystackSpan

# Install: pip install haystack-ai vex-sdk

# Zero-config integration
vex = VexObserver(api_key="vex-your-key")

rag_pipeline = Pipeline()
rag_pipeline.add_component("retriever", InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever())
rag_pipeline.add_component("generator", OpenAIGenerator())

# Vex auto-instruments the entire pipeline
@VexObserver.trace_pipeline(vex)
def monitored_rag(query: str):
    result = rag_pipeline.run({
        "retriever": {"query": query},
        "generator": {"prompt": "Answer based on context: {{context}}")
    })
    # Vex auto-generates spans for type mismatches, low relevance, hallucinations
    return result

# Production monitoring
spans: List[HaystackSpan] = vex.capture_pipeline(rag_pipeline)
vex.alert_on_failures(spans, thresholds={
    'retriever_relevance': 0.3,
    'generator_hallucination': 0.4,
    'pipeline_latency': '2s'
})

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add one line of Vex to your Haystack pipelines and eliminate 95% of agent failures automatically.
